title: France's Iranian community torn between republicans and monarchists
subhead: Many Iranians in France hope for regime change back home. However, those in favor of democracy are losing ground to supporters of a return to the monarchy, led by the son of Iran's former shah.

title: Netanyahu vows to carry on war, 'eradicate Iranian regime'
subhead: Israeli leader Benjamin Netanyahu said that thanks to the bombing campaign by Israeli and American pilots, 'We have gained almost complete control of the airspace' over Iran's capital. Speaking to the Iranian people, he said the 'moment of truth' was coming as Israel was seeking to help liberate them from the 'yoke of tyranny.'

title: Cultural heritage is under threat from the Middle East war
subhead: Several sites, including Tehran's Golestan Palace and Tel Aviv's Bauhaus buildings, have sustained damaged from missile strikes.

title: Formula 1 reshuffles the deck after major regulatory overhaul
subhead: With completely redesigned single-seaters following a sweeping change in regulations, Formula 1 drivers are kicking off the 2026 season on Sunday at the Australian Grand Prix. Despite ongoing uncertainty, McLaren aims to defend its titles against Mercedes, Red Bull and Ferrari.

title: Tracey Emin has art in her blood: 'Each of her works is both testimony and proof that she survived and has been able to give form to trauma'
subhead: A rebellious and accomplished figure on the British art scene, Emin, who has been affected by illness, has returned to live in Margate, the seaside resort where she grew up. The artist explains how she is developing a microcosm of galleries, schools and studios there, aimed at fostering a new generation of creators. She also looks back on her chaotic career, as the Tate Modern dedicates a retrospective to her.

Iranian school hit by air strike: Le Monde's investigation confirms many civilian victims, including children
subhead: Videos obtained and analyzed by Le Monde confirmed that the bombing of a school in Minab, Iran, on the first day of joint US-Israeli strikes on the country, caused many civilian victims, including children.
byline: Alexandre Horn,  Asia Balluffier and  Ghazal Golshiri

title: Azerbaijan and Armenia, both neighbors of Iran, fear regional spillover of conflict
subhead: Two Iranian drones struck Azerbaijan's Nakhchivan region on Thursday, raising fears of an expansion of hostilities in the South Caucasus, as Baku maintains close ties with Israel.

title: War in Iran puts political pressure on both isolationist Vance and interventionist Rubio
subhead: Neither the US vice president nor the secretary of state stands to gain from a show of military force that lacks a clear strategy and runs counter to the MAGA doctrine. The conflict is primarily Donald Trump's, who appears unconcerned about his political legacy.
